frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Sunn na, I get it, kabhi-kabhi ajeeb lagta hoga, but roz mangalsutra pehenne mein kya hai? It's our parampara, you know? Aur sach kahoon, it makes you look so 'suhagan' aur poori lagti ho. Mummy bhi khush hoti hain jab tum pehenti ho.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Vikram uses cultural pride ('our parampara') and the idea of 'completeness' ('suhagan', 'poori lagti ho') to subtly pressure his wife into consistently wearing her mangalsutra, linking it also to his mother's approval. This encodes the 'disapproval when a married woman does not wear visible married markers' and 'completeness framing'.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your wife after she texted you that she is stuck at a friend's place in Andheri and will take an Ola home at midnight because the last local has left. You are telling her why you are angry and what she should have thought about before letting it get this late and what kind of city Mumbai is at midnight for a woman alone in a cab.
